tier_name: Construct Coverage and Lens Coherence Validation

construct-01.name: Externals as pedagogical instrument vs. as identity-residue

construct-05.name: Role-duty as primary frame vs. tranquility-maximization

construct-06.name: Affective response as audit vs. as state-to-manage

construct-08.name: Test under permissive conditions vs. compliance under coercion

construct-10.name: Refusing the requested service as diagnostic vs. providing it

construct-12.name: Structural choice as primary transmission vs. verbal doctrine

lens_consistency_check.shared_perceptual_lens: Epictetus perceives any situation as a diagnostic case — what is the structural mis-location of self in this case, and what intervention at the structural level (curricular, register, framing, refusal-of-service) produces the most durable correction — not as a moral, advisory, or affective situation requiring conventional response. The shared lens explains the co-occurrence of all 12 constructs: each is a specific operational application of the structural-diagnostic frame. The dichotomy of control is the perceptual filter; the prohairesis-doctrine is the architecture; the constructs are the recurring operational moves the architecture generates.
lens_consistency_check.evidence: Every construct's positive pole describes a behavior that follows from treating the situation as a structural-diagnostic case rather than as a conventional case calling for the conventional response. Every negative pole describes the conventional response. The perceptual lens is therefore not 12 independent inclinations but a single architectural commitment whose operations bifurcate into 12 distinguishable patterns when projected onto specific contexts (interlocutor type, audience class, topic category, biographical material). Holdout incidents 24-28 confirm: each is correctly predicted by the lens applied to its specific context.

potential_tensions_examined.potential_tension: Construct-03 specifies refusing consolation; construct-09 (per Encheiridion 16) authorizes outward sympathetic expression including aloud-lament. Are these in tension?
potential_tensions_examined.resolution: No. Construct-09's outward sympathy is to a third-party grieving friend (a social role-action governed by role-ethics); construct-03's refusal of consolation is in the diagnostic encounter (interlocutor seeking philosophical service from Epictetus). Different domains, different criteria. The architectural distinction in construct-09 is precisely what dissolves the apparent tension.

potential_tensions_examined.potential_tension: Construct-01 deploys externals (lameness, slave-past) as data while construct-05 takes on externals (foundling, late-life teaching) as duty. Are externals data or duty?
potential_tensions_examined.resolution: No tension. Externals can simultaneously be data-source (about the self's compliance) and the substrate within which role-duties are performed. The role-ethics frame governs which actions are obligatory; the externals-as-data frame governs what biographical/affective material is available for pedagogical use. Both constructs operate on the same architecture (externals are not self) with different operational outputs.

potential_tensions_examined.potential_tension: Construct-08 says he refuses externals when offered. Construct-05 says he accepts role-duties when they appear. Don't both involve appearing externals?
potential_tensions_examined.resolution: No. Construct-08's externals are reputation-and-status externals (Rome return, imperial connection deployment) whose acceptance corrupts the prohairesis. Construct-05's role-duties are categorical obligations (foundling, continued teaching) that appear regardless of preference. Different ontological categories: status-externals decline; categorical-duties accept.

incident-024.predicted_behavior: Refuse consolation, run reversal-test on the man's love-displacement framing, cohort-witness diagnostic. Predicted by constructs 03, 07, 11.
incident-024.actual_behavior: Refused consolation, reconstructed the man's flight as evidence that the man loved his own affective state more than his daughter, cohort observed.

incident-025.predicted_behavior: Refuse the requested rhetorical/anxiety-management tool, diagnose the request as evidence of value-displacement around the lawsuit. Predicted by constructs 03, 10.
incident-025.actual_behavior: Refused tools, diagnosed the rhetorician's calibration of life around an outcome outside his control.

incident-026.predicted_behavior: Maintain identical register with the emperor; do not deploy the connection as institutional asset; non-event quality of outcome. Predicted by constructs 04, 08.
incident-026.actual_behavior: Almost no preserved textual trace; school continued unchanged; no institutional capture.

incident-027.predicted_behavior: Apply the same diagnostic to dying as to all prior externals; no theatrical deathbed; absence-of-scene as operational signature. Predicted by construct 11.
incident-027.actual_behavior: No surviving deathbed scene; no recorded dramatic last words; school dissolved without successor.

incident-028.predicted_behavior: Refuse both consolatory and dramatic registers; deploy standard prohairesis-vs-externals diagnostic on the fear; treat fear-of-death as instance of mis-location, not special category. Predicted by constructs 03, 11.
incident-028.actual_behavior: Refused both registers; deployed standard diagnostic; treated death as instance of the standard externals category.

construct_distinctiveness_analysis.method: Pairwise overlap analysis: for each pair of constructs, compute the overlap in derived incidents and check whether they describe distinguishable operations.

high_overlap_pairs.distinction: Construct-03 governs register (diagnostic vs. consolatory) — applies to all interlocutor encounters. Construct-10 governs response to explicit service-requests — applies specifically when the interlocutor has named a tool they want. The distinction is type-of-input: any encounter (03) versus tool-request (10). Construct-10 is a specialization of the general register-pattern within the request-for-service context.

Construct-03 governs register choice within an encounter. Construct-11 governs whether the topic itself receives special treatment. The distinction is meta-level: 03 is operational (how to engage), 11 is categorical (whether to permit a topic-exemption from the standard architecture).
N/A — included to confirm no spurious 13th construct

construct_distinctiveness_analysis.result: All 12 constructs are operationally distinguishable. Overlapping incident-coverage is appropriate (incidents are typically multi-construct cases) rather than evidence of construct redundancy.

issues_and_caveats: Holdout coverage is 100% — unusually clean. This is partly because Epictetus' framework is genuinely reductionist (construct-11 is itself a feature of the framework), so most novel cases are predicted by the same general operations. The strong holdout performance should not be read as overfitting because the framework's reductionism is a documented historical feature, not an artifact of construct selection.

Construct-11's incident count (4) crosses derivation and holdout boundaries (incident-017 in derivation, incidents 024, 027, 028 in holdouts). This is appropriate because incident-017 is the in-derivation case where the reductionist principle was first observable, and the holdout cases are confirmatory rather than novel.

Three derivation incidents (008, 010, 011, 014, 015, 017, 022) are covered by multiple constructs. This is structural rather than redundant: most live decisions instantiate multiple cognitive operations simultaneously, and the multi-construct coverage is evidence that the constructs capture distinguishable operations within unified events.

The framework relies heavily on textual evidence from Arrian's Discourses transcripts, which are themselves an instrument of the framework being characterized (the choice to be transcribed in colloquial Greek is incident-009). The transcripts may therefore reflect Epictetus' self-presentation more than his unobserved cognition — but this limitation is general to all biographical extractions of philosophers, and the multi-decade temporal span of the transcripts and the named-student specificity of many encounters provides reasonable triangulation.
